channel.title: Tianyu Xiang
channel.description: Academic website of Tianyu Xiang — multimodal human manipulation modeling, online neural decoding, and brain-inspired human–robot skill transfer.

item.title: I joined The Pennsylvania State University as a postdoctoral researcher in the Penn State Neuromechanics Laboratory.
item.description: I joined The Pennsylvania State University as a postdoctoral researcher in the Penn State Neuromechanics Laboratory.

item.title: I received the Chinese Academy of Sciences President’s Special Award, the Academy’s highest honor for graduate students.
item.description: I received the Chinese Academy of Sciences President’s Special Award, the Academy’s highest honor for graduate students.

item.title: I completed my Ph.D. in Pattern Recognition and Intelligent Systems with a dissertation on multimodal human manipulation representation and brain-inspired human–robot skill transfer.
item.description: I completed my Ph.D. in Pattern Recognition and Intelligent Systems with a dissertation on multimodal human manipulation representation and brain-inspired human–robot skill transfer.

item.title: Our paper “Task-Oriented Learning for Automatic EEG Denoising” was accepted by IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement.
item.description: Our paper “Task-Oriented Learning for Automatic EEG Denoising” was accepted by IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement.

item.title: Our paper “Cross-Dataset Montage Alignment via Electroencephalogram Source Imaging for Enhanced Motor Decoding” was accepted by IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems.
item.description: Our paper “Cross-Dataset Montage Alignment via Electroencephalogram Source Imaging for Enhanced Motor Decoding” was accepted by IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems.

item.title: Our proposal on brain-inspired computational intelligence for human learning modeling received an IEEE CIS Graduate Student Research Grant.
item.description: Our proposal on brain-inspired computational intelligence for human learning modeling received an IEEE CIS Graduate Student Research Grant.
